title: mirabilis laevis
intro: Mirabilis laevis, also known as the yellow water lily or western water lily, is a perennial aquatic plant native to western North America. The roots and rhizomes of Mirabilis laevis contain medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant compounds, which have been used to treat various health conditions. The plant has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, offering health benefits such as reducing inflammation and promoting wound healing. Herbal preparations of Mirabilis laevis, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, can be made from the roots and rhizomes, although its use should be under the guidance of a qualified healthcare professional.
